evals.prompt: make test-race is failing in my CI pipeline, how do I run it locally to reproduce the race condition?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user to run make test-race from the repo root, and shows the equivalent go test -v -race ./... command for more targeted investigation.
assertions: Response includes make test-race as the primary command
Response includes the equivalent go test -v -race ./... command
Response says to run the command from the repository root

evals.prompt: the CI coverage check is failing, how do I see which functions are pulling the coverage below 80%?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user to run make test-coverage to generate coverage.out, then inspect it with go tool cover -func=coverage.out to see per-function coverage. Also mentions make test-coverage-check enforces the 80% threshold.
assertions: Response includes go tool cover -func=coverage.out to inspect per-function coverage
Response mentions make test-coverage to generate the coverage.out file
Response mentions the 80% threshold enforced by make test-coverage-check

evals.prompt: make check is failing with an error saying a linter or static analysis tool is missing — how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Tells the user to run make dev-setup to install the required static analysis/dev tools, then retry make check.
assertions: Response includes make dev-setup as the fix
Response does NOT suggest manually running go install for individual tools as the primary fix
Response mentions retrying make check after running dev-setup
